rulings.Case Title: 1429 GRANT AVE, LLC, VS. ANDREW LINTON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, Sep. 02, 2021, line 1. PLAINTIFF 1429 GRANT AVE, LLC's MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. DENIED. Request for judicial notice is granted and the Court will take notice of the existence of the bankruptcy filings and Deeds. The Court will not take judicial notice of the facts asserted in Form 6A. (RJN 2). Plaintiff relies solely on assertions in that Form to show that Bannam Place, LLC was still the owner of the Property as of June 9, 2014, and the bankruptcy debtor was 100% owner of the Bannam Place, LLC as of that date. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.= (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: CONSTANCE FINLEY VS. MITCHELL HUANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, Sep. 02, 2021, line 2. 2-DEFENDANT COURTNEY DAVIS, PARK NORTH REAL ESTATE's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Adjudication. Continued to September 12, 2021 for Plaintiff to file a supplemental brief limited solely to listing evidence supporting fraud (not negligence) re: CCP 338(d) statute of limitations applicability. Supplemental brief shall only include evidence in the following format: (a) title of document; (b) page; (c) line number. For example (1) Finley Declaration 7:22-25; (2) RJN, Ex. 2, page 3, line 17. No further argument permitted. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.= (501/CFH)
